reviewer_objection: Why is shard 008 publishable at all if it still has only one document-grade procedural anchor and two same-family Springer direct notices?
bounded_response_now: Because the current publishable unit is an evidence-architecture panel, not a prevalence panel. Table 1E now makes seven distinct source-depth states legible: one Italian issuing-authority order, one Finnish direct-source-resolved multilingual comparator with official-probe limits, one mediated editor-statement row, one blocked retraction-DOI row, two direct Springer-originating notice-text exemplars on different venue types, and one mixed residual floor.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: publishable as a source-depth and claim-ceiling demonstration, not as a count, prevalence, sanction, or system-wide legal-process package.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Table 1E reviewer-warning note; source-depth ladder; thesis claim-boundary appendix.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the package bounded until one Finnish official companion or one non-Springer direct notice changes the current ceiling.

reviewer_objection: Is the Finland row being overread as official confirmation of AI-assisted administrative appeals?
bounded_response_now: No. The strongest safe reading is still one direct-source-resolved public-broadcaster row naming institutions and workload effects, plus an explicit official-probe ladder that records actor-role verification, governance-context pages, judicial search-surface friction, and several content-bearing official police PDFs that were opened and ruled out as context-only or false-positive hits.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: Finland remains below official phenomenon confirmation and below a second document-grade procedural family.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Authority-escalation ladder; keyword-collision guardrail panel; jurisdiction/language caution overlay.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Capture one Finnish police, court, ministry, or judicial-process document that independently confirms the phenomenon or procedural response.

reviewer_objection: Why keep the NEJM and SAGE rows visible if the publisher pages are blocked from this lane?
bounded_response_now: Because blocked-but-verified routes are still useful methods evidence. The package can already distinguish verified article identity plus named-editor quotation (SAGE) and verified retraction DOI-pair metadata (NEJM) from direct notice text, and the blocked DOI landings document why those rows stay below direct-notice state instead of being silently dropped or overstated.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: blocked access remains a visible limitation and does not justify inferring publisher reasoning or final editorial action.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Blocked-notice queue; source-friction facet; notice-depth funnel.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Upgrade only if a materially different public publisher-originating surface or equivalent public record appears.

reviewer_objection: Why keep a residual bucket instead of showing only the cleaner direct-notice and comparator rows?
bounded_response_now: Because the mixed residual floor is itself reviewer-relevant negative evidence. One residual row has escaped into an archived SSRN platform-action record, but two others still remain specialist-report-only or unresolved discovery surfaces; hiding the bucket would falsely imply that the whole research-integrity tail has already hardened into case-grade action records.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: the residual bucket remains mixed and quarantined; no plagiarism finding, sanction, or full-case closure may be inferred from the archived SSRN page alone.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Residual-category panel; blocked-source queue; source-family saturation map.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Capture one second residual direct action record or one stronger non-Springer direct notice before softening residual-floor caution.

reviewer_objection: Does the Springer book notice solve shard 008's publisher-diversity problem?
bounded_response_now: No. It improves venue-type breadth by adding a book-surface direct notice alongside the already captured Springer journal notice, but both top-depth direct-notice exemplars still sit inside the Springer family. That means Table 1E can now defend journal-versus-book heterogeneity, not cross-publisher recurrence.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: venue-type breadth improves while publisher diversity remains open.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Venue-type comparison card; notice-text funnel; metadata-consistency facet.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one non-Springer direct journal or publisher notice before any stronger comparative rhetoric.

reviewer_objection: What is the smallest next move that strengthens shard 008 more than another packaging pass?
bounded_response_now: A threshold-changing source gain, not another rebuttal layer. The best dual-payoff move is still one Finnish official companion because it would strengthen both procedural-family depth and under-covered-language support; the next best research-integrity move is one non-Springer direct notice, with NEJM blocked-route breakthrough still high-value but less feasible from this lane.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: route order remains threshold-sensitive and the current package should stay bounded until one of those source gains lands.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Priority ladder widget; reviewer-response closing note; dashboard next-upgrade card.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one real authority-changing or publisher-diversifying document over another packaging-only shard-008 memo.
